name: Gemima
short_description: Sokoban, shared! A cute, two-player co-op twist on classic Sokoban-style gameplay. Or play solo for a real test of dexterity!
detailed_description:
A Fresh Twist On Classic Block-Pushing Gameplay

Gemima brings infuriatingly compelling couch co-op to the classic Sokoban genre! Turns out life in four directons isn't necessarily simpler...

Twice The Challenge, Twice The Fun!

Grab a friend (or play solo for a true test of dexterity!) and take control of apprentices Gemima and Jules in their quest to gather gemstones from the Mine of Mages. They'll have to work together closely to overcome the many traps and obstacles, and use their magical affinities (helpfully depicted by their hair colour) to dig, push, and collect their way to success.

Play Against The Clock - Or Not

Take your time and think things through - or for the ultimate challenge, coordinate to solve each level before the Mage Mining Company blows the tunnels sky-high.

Oodles Of Levels - And A Map To Explore!

Many puzzles have more than one solution, and each will take you in a different direction. Stuck on one puzzle? Explore somewhere else! Can you find every nook and cranny of the mine?

Co-op On One Keyboard Or Controller

Gemima's simple controls mean two players can share a keyboard or controller. Although that will put you within easy reach when you make a mistake...

Difficulty and Longevity

Puzzles in Gemima range in difficulty from moderate to very challenging. The game has been completed (minus secrets) in 6 hours - do not expect to replicate that. A more typical time for a playthrough is around 20 hours.
